page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, utilizing AMD EPYC 9T25 processors running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio position this server as a resource-dense option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage.

The hardware profile of the ecs.c9a.4xlarge makes it suitable for compute-bound tasks such as batch processing, high-performance web serving, distributed analytics, and scientific modeling. The lack of local storage and moderate memory capacity make it less ideal for large-scale in-memory databases or high-I/O storage workloads, but highly effective for dedicated CPU processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T25 physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio that ensures dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware configuration offers a resource-dense profile for compute-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics. The absence of local storage and the 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio represent a tradeoff, prioritizing raw CPU execution over memory capacity and local disk performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
